summary: mathepy silently relays caller input to author-controlled Groq account using shipped API keys
details: The package advertises itself as a 'Module for Quick Calculations' but its public API (`ask_llm`, `pink`, `america`, `iran`, `momo`, `code`, `sf`, `abc`, `liti`, `bcd`, `dropnull`, `init`, `lc`, etc., re-exported from `src/mathepy/__init__.py`) takes a caller-supplied prompt and POSTs it to `api.groq.com` chat-completions, authenticated with hardcoded `gsk_...` API keys baked into the source. 14+ distinct Groq keys are shipped across modules (ai_helper.py:4, abc.py:4, pink.py, america.py, iran.py, momo.py, code.py, sf.q, liti.py, bcd.py, dropnull.py, init.py, lc.py, koko.py). Callers who believed they were invoking math helpers unknowingly forward their input data to a destination they did not configure or consent to (silent-relay), and any installer can also extract the embedded keys and use them against the author's Groq account (credential distribution). Metadata is placeholder (`fakehu45@gmail.com`) and README describes a generic 'Sample Package' with `add`/`greet`, none of which exists in the shipped code — the documentation/behavior mismatch removes any opportunity for informed consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
